summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. This record now validates Microsoft's guidance that switching between Modern Standby and S3 is not a supported Windows setting change. The current app's registry bundle remains documented as an implementation mismatch rather than a supported control.

setting.name: Modern Standby Power Model
setting.casual_explanation: Modern Standby is the low-power idle sleep model used on many newer systems. This tweak tries to turn it off and push the system back toward traditional S3-style sleep behavior.
setting.why_people_change_it: People try to disable Modern Standby when they want classic sleep behavior or when they suspect Modern Standby is involved in battery drain, wake, or sleep issues.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The feature is real, but Microsoft's guidance explicitly says switching power models is not supported by changing a setting in Windows. That makes this registry-based tweak too risky to validate.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Modern Standby documentation states that switching between S3 and Modern Standby cannot be done by changing a setting and is not supported in Windows without a complete OS reinstall. The current app writes MSDisabled and ModernSleep\EnabledActions registry values, but this review did not find a primary Microsoft registry mapping that blesses those writes as a supported transition path.

allowed_values.label: Unsupported Windows-side switch
allowed_values.meaning: The official Microsoft guidance used in this review does not support switching between Modern Standby and S3 through a registry setting.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes MSDisabled = 1 and ModernSleep\EnabledActions = 0, but the Microsoft guidance used here says switching power models is not supported by changing a Windows setting.

windows_defaults.label: Hardware and image dependent baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems whose shipped platform design supports either Modern Standby or S3

states.rationale: The shipped sleep model depends on platform support and image design, not on a validated post-install registry toggle.

windows_defaults.notes: This review follows Microsoft's guidance that switching power models is not a supported Windows setting change.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows shipped baseline
intended_for: Systems staying on their supported sleep model
avoid_for: None until a supported Microsoft transition path exists

states.rationale: Stay on the system's supported shipped sleep model.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stay aligned with Microsoft's supported behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to use an undocumented registry path to force a power-model change.
tradeoffs: The system stays on its supported sleep model.
The current registry-based tweak remains documented but not validated.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: What is Modern Standby
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents Modern Standby and states that switching between S3 and Modern Standby is not supported in Windows without a complete OS reinstall.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes MSDisabled = 1 and ModernSleep\EnabledActions = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Repo power notes
evidence.summary: The repo tracks these registry values as power research, but this review did not promote them to a supported Microsoft transition mechanism.

decision.why: Microsoft's guidance is now validated in this record, and it does not support switching between Modern Standby and S3 through a Windows setting change. The record is documented for research, not as a supported Windows tweak.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Switching the power model is not supported in Windows without a complete OS re-install.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft documents that switching between S3 and Modern Standby is not supported through a Windows setting change.
